# MCP Echo Server Error Handling Tests
# Tests various error conditions and invalid inputs

# Build the echo server binary
go build -o mcp-echo-server .

# Test missing message parameter
exec mcp-echo-server <<EOF
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2024-11-05","capabilities":{}},"id":1}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"initialized","params":{}}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"echo","arguments":{}},"id":2}
EOF

stderr 'missing required argument: message'

# Test empty message parameter
exec mcp-echo-server <<EOF
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2024-11-05","capabilities":{}},"id":1}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"initialized","params":{}}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"echo","arguments":{"message":""}},"id":2}
EOF

stderr 'message cannot be empty'

# Test message with only whitespace
exec mcp-echo-server <<EOF
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2024-11-05","capabilities":{}},"id":1}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"initialized","params":{}}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"echo","arguments":{"message":"   "}},"id":2}
EOF

stderr 'message cannot be empty'

# Test message with tabs and newlines only
exec mcp-echo-server <<EOF
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2024-11-05","capabilities":{}},"id":1}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"initialized","params":{}}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"echo","arguments":{"message":"\t\n\r "}},"id":2}
EOF

stderr 'message cannot be empty'

# Test invalid message type (number instead of string)
exec mcp-echo-server <<EOF
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2024-11-05","capabilities":{}},"id":1}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"initialized","params":{}}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"echo","arguments":{"message":123}},"id":2}
EOF

stderr 'invalid message argument'

# Test invalid message type (boolean instead of string)
exec mcp-echo-server <<EOF
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2024-11-05","capabilities":{}},"id":1}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"initialized","params":{}}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"echo","arguments":{"message":true}},"id":2}
EOF

stderr 'invalid message argument'

# Test invalid message type (array instead of string)
exec mcp-echo-server <<EOF
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2024-11-05","capabilities":{}},"id":1}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"initialized","params":{}}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"echo","arguments":{"message":["array","value"]}},"id":2}
EOF

stderr 'invalid message argument'

# Test invalid message type (object instead of string)
exec mcp-echo-server <<EOF
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2024-11-05","capabilities":{}},"id":1}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"initialized","params":{}}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"echo","arguments":{"message":{"nested":"object"}}},"id":2}
EOF

stderr 'invalid message argument'

# Test invalid message type (null)
exec mcp-echo-server <<EOF
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2024-11-05","capabilities":{}},"id":1}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"initialized","params":{}}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"echo","arguments":{"message":null}},"id":2}
EOF

stderr 'invalid message argument'

# Test malformed JSON in arguments
exec mcp-echo-server <<EOF
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2024-11-05","capabilities":{}},"id":1}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"initialized","params":{}}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"echo","arguments":"invalid"},"id":2}
EOF

stderr 'json: cannot unmarshal string into Go value of type'

# Test calling non-existent tool
exec mcp-echo-server <<EOF
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2024-11-05","capabilities":{}},"id":1}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"initialized","params":{}}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"nonexistent","arguments":{"message":"test"}},"id":2}
EOF

stderr 'tool not found'

# Test with extra unknown parameters (should still work)
exec mcp-echo-server <<EOF
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2024-11-05","capabilities":{}},"id":1}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"initialized","params":{}}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"echo","arguments":{"message":"test","extra_param":"ignored"}},"id":2}
EOF

stdout '"echo":"test"'
stdout '"timestamp"'
